title: pimelea curviflora subsp. gracilis
intro: Pimelea curviflora subsp. gracilis is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae, commonly known as the curved pimelea. It is a shrub native to Western Australia that has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including wound healing and the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema. The herbal preparations of Pimelea curviflora subsp. gracilis include infusions and poultices made from the leaves, which can be used topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
